openmeter/entitlement/adapter.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL adapter implementing entitlement.EntitlementRepo and balanceworker.BalanceWorkerRepository for the entitlement domain. All DB access goes through this package; it is the sole persistence layer for entitlement rows and usage-reset records.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo wrapping
patterns.description: Every method body is wrapped with entutils.TransactingRepo or TransactingRepoWithNoValue so the ctx-bound Ent transaction is honored. Never call repo.db.* directly in the method body.
patterns.example: entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context, repo *entitlementDBAdapter) (*entitlement.Entitlement, error) { ... })

patterns.name: TxUser + TxCreator implementation in transaction.go
patterns.description: entitlementDBAdapter and usageResetDBAdapter each implement Tx(), WithTx(), and Self() in transaction.go, enabling entutils to rebind to an existing ctx transaction or start a new one.
patterns.example: func (e *entitlementDBAdapter) WithTx(ctx context.Context, tx *entutils.TxDriver) *entitlementDBAdapter { txClient := db.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()); return NewPostgresEntitlementRepo(txClient.Client()) }

patterns.name: Soft-delete filter
patterns.description: Deleted entitlements are excluded by appending db_entitlement.Or(db_entitlement.DeletedAtGT(now), db_entitlement.DeletedAtIsNil()). Never omit this filter when querying active entitlements.
patterns.example: query.Where(db_entitlement.Or(db_entitlement.DeletedAtGT(now), db_entitlement.DeletedAtIsNil()))

patterns.name: withAllUsageResets helper
patterns.description: List/Get queries call withAllUsageResets(...) to eager-load usage reset edges needed for UsagePeriod calculations. Omitting this causes mapEntitlementEntity to fail.
patterns.example: withAllUsageResets(repo.db.Entitlement.Query(), []string{namespace}).Where(...)

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertion
patterns.description: var _ repo = (*entitlementDBAdapter)(nil) and var _ interface{TxCreator; TxUser} = (*entitlementDBAdapter)(nil) guard interface compliance at compile time.
patterns.example: var _ repo = (*entitlementDBAdapter)(nil)

key_file_guides.role: Main adapter: implements all entitlement.EntitlementRepo and balanceworker.BalanceWorkerRepository methods using Ent queries.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any new method must use TransactingRepo; always include withAllUsageResets for queries that map to domain Entitlement structs.

key_file_guides.role: Implements Tx/WithTx/Self for both entitlementDBAdapter and usageResetDBAdapter. Required by entutils transaction machinery.
key_file_guides.watch_for: If you add a new adapter struct, copy this pattern or entutils will panic.

key_file_guides.role: usageResetDBAdapter implementing meteredentitlement.UsageResetRepo.Save — writes a usage_reset row inside a transaction.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must call usageResetTime.Validate() before the Ent create call.

key_file_guides.role: Integration tests for the adapter using a real Postgres DB provisioned by testutils.InitPostgresDB. Constructs adapters directly, not through app/common.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses context.Background() — ok in tests; do not copy this pattern into production code.

anti_patterns: Calling repo.db.* directly inside a method body without TransactingRepo wrapping.
Constructing adapters via app/common in tests — build directly from NewPostgresEntitlementRepo.
Omitting withAllUsageResets on any query that feeds mapEntitlementEntity.
Adding business logic to the adapter — keep it pure persistence.
Using context.Background() inside production adapter methods.

decisions.decision: Two separate adapters (entitlementDBAdapter, usageResetDBAdapter) each with their own TxUser implementation.
decisions.rationale: Ent transactions are client-scoped; splitting keeps each struct small and avoids mixing unrelated schema edges in the same rebind path.

decisions.decision: Soft-delete via DeletedAt timestamp rather than hard delete.
decisions.rationale: Entitlement history is needed for billing reconciliation; hard deletes would destroy audit trails.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new write method to entitlementDBAdapter

openmeter/entitlement/boolean.purpose: Boolean entitlement sub-type: implements entitlement.SubTypeConnector for on/off access entitlements that carry no balance or usage tracking. Single responsibility: validate creation inputs and always return HasAccess=true.

patterns.name: SubTypeConnector implementation
patterns.description: Implement entitlement.SubTypeConnector (GetValue, BeforeCreate, AfterCreate). BeforeCreate validates that no metered fields (MeasureUsageFrom, IssueAfterReset, IsSoftLimit, Config) are set and returns CreateEntitlementRepoInputs.
patterns.example: func (c *connector) BeforeCreate(model entitlement.CreateEntitlementInputs, feature feature.Feature) (*entitlement.CreateEntitlementRepoInputs, error) { ... }

patterns.name: ParseFromGenericEntitlement type guard
patterns.description: ParseFromGenericEntitlement checks EntitlementType == EntitlementTypeBoolean and returns WrongTypeError if not. Always call this at the top of GetValue.
patterns.example: _, err := ParseFromGenericEntitlement(entitlement); if err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: AfterCreate no-op
patterns.description: AfterCreate returns nil immediately — boolean entitlements require no post-creation side effects (no default grant, no balance snapshot).
patterns.example: func (c *connector) AfterCreate(ctx context.Context, entitlement *entitlement.Entitlement) error { return nil }

key_file_guides.role: Full SubTypeConnector implementation: GetValue always returns BooleanEntitlementValue{HasAccess: true}; BeforeCreate rejects metered-only fields.
key_file_guides.watch_for: UsagePeriod IS allowed for boolean entitlements — BeforeCreate computes currentUsagePeriod if UsagePeriod is set.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Entitlement struct (embeds GenericProperties) and ParseFromGenericEntitlement type-narrowing function.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Struct has no extra fields beyond GenericProperties — do not add balance fields here.

anti_patterns: Adding balance or usage fields to the boolean Entitlement struct.
Returning HasAccess=false from BooleanEntitlementValue — boolean access is always true.
Calling the credit engine from AfterCreate — boolean entitlements have no grants.

decisions.decision: Boolean entitlement always returns HasAccess=true without any credit computation.
decisions.rationale: Boolean entitlements are pure feature flags; there is no concept of credit burn-down, so the value is always true as long as the entitlement exists and is active.

openmeter/entitlement/metered.purpose: Metered entitlement sub-type implementing credit-backed grant burn-down tracking. Owns balance queries (GetEntitlementBalance, GetEntitlementBalanceHistory), usage resets, grant lifecycle (CreateGrant, ListEntitlementGrants), and the grant.OwnerConnector bridge that feeds the credit engine. This is the most complex sub-type.

patterns.name: connector struct wires credit sub-systems
patterns.description: connector holds streamingConnector, ownerConnector, balanceConnector, grantConnector, grantRepo, entitlementRepo. NewMeteredEntitlementConnector takes all of these; Wire provides them. Never instantiate connector directly in production code.
patterns.example: meteredentitlement.NewMeteredEntitlementConnector(streamingConnector, ownerConnector, balanceConnector, grantConnector, grantRepo, entitlementRepo, publisher, logger, tracer)

patterns.name: ResetEntitlementUsage runs inside a transaction
patterns.description: ResetEntitlementUsage calls transaction.Run(ctx, e.grantRepo, ...) to ensure the balance reset and usage-reset row write are atomic. Hooks and event publish happen inside the same transaction closure.
patterns.example: return transaction.Run(ctx, e.grantRepo, func(ctx context.Context) (*EntitlementBalance, error) { ... e.balanceConnector.ResetUsageForOwner ... e.publisher.Publish ... })

patterns.name: ParseFromGenericEntitlement strict type guard
patterns.description: Every connector method that works with a specific sub-type calls ParseFromGenericEntitlement immediately and returns WrongTypeError if the type doesn't match. Required fields (MeasureUsageFrom, IsSoftLimit, UsagePeriod, LastReset, CurrentUsagePeriod, OriginalUsagePeriodAnchor) are validated.
patterns.example: mEnt, err := ParseFromGenericEntitlement(ent); if err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: EndCurrentUsagePeriod requires an active transaction
patterns.description: EndCurrentUsagePeriod checks transaction.GetDriverFromContext before running — it must be called in a transaction. Callers (balance-worker reset path) are responsible for starting the transaction first.
patterns.example: _, err := transaction.GetDriverFromContext(ctx); if err != nil { return fmt.Errorf("end current usage period must be called in a transaction: %w", err) }

patterns.name: Event versioning: always emit V3 events
patterns.description: EntitlementResetEvent (v1) is deprecated; new code must emit EntitlementResetEventV3 which carries CustomerID instead of Subject. Events implement marshaler.Event with pinned event name including version.
patterns.example: event := EntitlementResetEventV3{EntitlementID: ..., CustomerID: ent.CustomerID, ...}; e.publisher.Publish(ctx, event)

patterns.name: ServiceHookRegistry for cross-domain lifecycle
patterns.description: connector embeds models.ServiceHookRegistry[Entitlement] and calls hooks.PreUpdate before mutations (CreateGrant, ResetEntitlementUsage). RegisterHooks is exposed on the Connector interface.
patterns.example: if err := e.hooks.PreUpdate(ctx, metered); err != nil { return EntitlementGrant{}, err }

key_file_guides.role: Defines Connector interface and struct, constructor, GetValue, BeforeCreate, AfterCreate (issues default grant if IssueAfterReset>0). Central orchestration file.
key_file_guides.watch_for: AfterCreate issues a default grant via grantConnector when HasDefaultGrant() is true — this happens outside a transaction; ensure idempotency.

key_file_guides.role: GetEntitlementBalance and GetEntitlementBalanceHistory. Balance queries round up to the nearest minute. History fills in zero-usage windows that ClickHouse omits.
key_file_guides.watch_for: queryMeter short-circuits to a zero row if from==to, preventing ClickHouse calls for zero-length periods.

key_file_guides.role: ResetEntitlementUsage and batch ResetEntitlementsWithExpiredUsagePeriod. Reset runs in transaction.Run wrapping grantRepo.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ResetEntitlementsWithExpiredUsagePeriod uses errors.Join to collect per-entitlement errors but continues iterating — partial failures are possible.

key_file_guides.role: entitlementGrantOwner implements grant.OwnerConnector: DescribeOwner resolves customer/meter/feature for a given entitlement ID; EndCurrentUsagePeriod writes a usage reset row and updates the stored current period.
key_file_guides.watch_for: getRepoMaybeInTx is a documented hack to rebind featureRepo to ctx transaction. LockOwnerForTx requires an active transaction in ctx.

key_file_guides.role: Metered-specific Entitlement struct with MeasureUsageFrom, IssueAfterReset, IsSoftLimit, UsagePeriod, CurrentUsagePeriod, LastReset. ParseFromGenericEntitlement does field-level nil checks.
key_file_guides.watch_for: IssueAfterResetPriority requires IssueAfterReset — ParseFromGenericEntitlement enforces this.

key_file_guides.role: EntitlementResetEvent (deprecated v1) and EntitlementResetEventV3. Both implement marshaler.Event with pinned EventName strings.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New events must implement Validate() and use metadata.GetEventName with a versioned EventType.

key_file_guides.role: UsageResetRepo interface and UsageResetUpdate input type with Validate(). UsageResetUpdate.UsagePeriodInterval must be a valid ISO duration.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Validate is called by the adapter before inserting; always keep it in sync with schema constraints.

anti_patterns: Bypassing transaction.Run in ResetEntitlementUsage — balance reset and event publish must be atomic.
Emitting EntitlementResetEvent (v1) from new code — use EntitlementResetEventV3.
Calling EndCurrentUsagePeriod outside of a transaction context.
Adding credit engine logic directly to connector methods without going through balanceConnector/grantConnector interfaces.
Using context.Background() in production connector methods — always propagate caller ctx.

decisions.decision: Balance history fills in zero-usage windows missing from ClickHouse results.
decisions.rationale: ClickHouse only returns rows with non-zero usage; the API contract requires a window entry for every time bucket in the queried range.

decisions.decision: ownerCustomer adapter in owner_customer.go wraps customer data without importing openmeter/customer into the credit package.
decisions.rationale: Prevents circular imports between meteredentitlement and credit packages while still satisfying streaming.Customer interface required by grant.Owner.

decisions.decision: IssueAfterReset with amount>0 causes AfterCreate to issue a default recurring grant.
decisions.rationale: Allows operators to configure a standing balance that automatically replenishes each usage period without manual grant operations.

code_examples.scenario: Set up and call the metered entitlement connector in tests
code_examples.code:
connector := meteredentitlement.NewMeteredEntitlementConnector(
	streamingConnector,
	ownerConnector,
	creditConnector, // implements credit.BalanceConnector
	creditConnector, // implements credit.GrantConnector
	grantRepo,
	entitlementRepo,
	mockPublisher,
	testLogger,
	tracer,
)
connector.RegisterHooks(
	meteredentitlement.ConvertHook(subscriptionHook),
)
balance, err := connector.GetEntitlementBalance(ctx,
	models.NamespacedID{Namespace: ns, ID: ent.ID}, clock.Now())

openmeter/entitlement/service.purpose: Orchestration layer implementing entitlement.Service by composing three sub-type connectors (metered, boolean, static), the feature connector, customer service, and a distributed locker. Routes entitlement operations to the correct sub-type and manages transaction boundaries for composite mutations.

patterns.name: ServiceConfig constructor pattern
patterns.description: NewEntitlementService takes a ServiceConfig struct (all deps explicit). Wire constructs ServiceConfig from individual providers; never add logic to the constructor.
patterns.example: entitlement.Service = service.NewEntitlementService(service.ServiceConfig{MeteredEntitlementConnector: ..., EntitlementRepo: ..., Locker: ...})

patterns.name: transaction.Run for composite mutations
patterns.description: CreateEntitlement and OverrideEntitlement wrap the full mutation (ScheduleEntitlement + CreateGrant loop) in transaction.Run to ensure atomicity.
patterns.example: return transaction.Run(ctx, c.entitlementRepo, func(ctx context.Context) (*entitlement.Entitlement, error) { ent, err := c.ScheduleEntitlement(ctx, input); ... })

patterns.name: ServiceHookRegistry fan-out
patterns.description: service embeds models.ServiceHookRegistry[entitlement.Entitlement] and exposes RegisterHooks. Hooks are called on mutations; balance-worker registers hooks to notify of balance changes.
patterns.example: s.hooks.RegisterHooks(hooks...)

patterns.name: registrybuilder for test setup
patterns.description: Tests use registrybuilder.GetEntitlementRegistry(EntitlementOptions{...}) to obtain a fully wired registry rather than importing app/common, avoiding import cycles.
patterns.example: entitlementRegistry := registrybuilder.GetEntitlementRegistry(registrybuilder.EntitlementOptions{DatabaseClient: dbClient, StreamingConnector: streamingConnector, ...})

key_file_guides.role: Concrete service implementation with all entitlement.Service methods. Dispatches to sub-type connectors via type switch or direct connector call.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreateEntitlement delegates to ScheduleEntitlement then loops over grants — if ScheduleEntitlement returns an error, no grants are issued.

key_file_guides.role: ScheduleEntitlement, SupersedeEntitlement and the time-window activation logic for scheduling future/expiring entitlements.
key_file_guides.watch_for: SupersedeEntitlement deletes the old entitlement and creates a new one in the same transaction.

key_file_guides.role: WithLock helper that acquires pg_advisory_xact_lock per entitlement/customer using lockr.Locker before mutation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Locker requires an active Postgres transaction; must be called after transaction.Run opens a tx.

anti_patterns: Importing app/common in test helpers — use registrybuilder instead.
Calling sub-type connector methods directly from HTTP handlers — always go through entitlement.Service.
Adding DB queries directly to service.go — all persistence goes through the EntitlementRepo interface.

decisions.decision: Service is a thin orchestrator that delegates to three independent sub-type connectors.
decisions.rationale: Each entitlement type has fundamentally different storage and computation needs; centralizing dispatch in one service avoids scatter of routing logic across HTTP handlers.

openmeter/entitlement/snapshot.purpose: Defines SnapshotEvent — the versioned Watermill event published to the system topic when an entitlement balance changes (reset, update, delete). Consumed by the balance-worker and notification-service.

patterns.name: marshaler.Event implementation
patterns.description: SnapshotEvent implements marshaler.Event (EventName, EventMetadata, Validate). EventName uses metadata.GetEventName with a pinned version string (v2). New snapshot event versions must bump the version and keep old versions for backward compatibility.
patterns.example: snapshotEventName = metadata.GetEventName(metadata.EventType{Subsystem: entitlement.EventSubsystem, Name: "entitlement.snapshot", Version: "v2"})

patterns.name: NewSnapshotEvent constructor
patterns.description: Use NewSnapshotEvent() to build SnapshotEvent rather than struct literals — it handles the deprecated Subject field safely when subj is nil.
patterns.example: snapshot.NewSnapshotEvent(ent, subj, customer, feat, snapshot.ValueOperationUpdate, &calculatedAt, &value, &currentUsagePeriod)

patterns.name: Validate enforces Value for update/reset operations
patterns.description: Validate() returns an error if Value is nil for ValueOperationUpdate or ValueOperationReset. Delete operations allow nil Value.
patterns.example: case ValueOperationUpdate, ValueOperationReset: if e.Value == nil { errs = append(errs, errors.New("balance is required for balance update/reset")) }

key_file_guides.role: Defines SnapshotEvent, EntitlementValue (balance/usage/overage/access fields), ValueOperationType enum, and NewSnapshotEvent constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Subject field is deprecated — new consumers should use Customer.ID. Set Subject to empty Subject{} if no usage attribution.

anti_patterns: Creating SnapshotEvent with struct literals that omit CalculatedAt — Validate() will reject it.
Bumping the event version in-place instead of adding a new versioned struct.
Reading Subject field in new consumer code — use Customer instead.

decisions.decision: SnapshotEvent v2 carries both Subject (deprecated) and Customer to maintain backward compatibility with existing consumers.
decisions.rationale: The balance-worker and edge workers were originally built around subjects; migrating them to customer-centric routing required keeping both fields during the transition.
